article_meta.title: Elections Unsealed Court Docs Show The FBI Probing Possible Election Crimes In Fulton County By: M.D

reasoning: The model does a generally good job identifying genuine bias in the author's own prose. Most flagged instances are defensible. There is one potential attribution issue with 'extremely sloppy' which the author uses but is quoting from a source (the independent monitor's review), though the author chose to feature it in the subheadline/lede framing. The model catches the major instances of author bias well. Some minor issues with overlapping flags and one debatable item.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The 'extremely sloppy' item is borderline - it's in quotes in the article and attributed to an independent monitor, though the author chose to feature it prominently in the subheadline. This is a minor attribution concern. The other flagged items are all clearly from the author's own prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible. The 'extremely sloppy' flag is questionable since it's attributed language. The Slant flag overlaps significantly with the Mudslinging flag on nearly the same text. The remaining items (Trump-hating publication, propaganda press, CEIR/Zuckbucks framing, nothing to hide, attack on election questioners) are all genuine author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches the major bias instances. It could have flagged 'Fulton County apologists' (Word Choice - dismissive label for those who disagree), and 'Not quite' (author directly editorializing to contradict a source). These are missed but the model caught the most prominent instances.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are generally reasonable. 'Elite vs. Populist Bias' for the CEIR/Zuckbucks passage is defensible but could also be Word Choice or Spin. The Sensationalism label for the 'nothing to hide' passage could also be Flawed Logic (argument from suspicion). Labels are close enough to be defensible.
